# Meetings

## Meeting Expectations

- There should always be a designated notetaker tracking decisions and next steps
- The most junior person in the meeting is responsible for taking notes unless otherwise noted
- Come prepared with the link to the notes document before the meeting starts
- All team members should participate actively and take responsibility for the usefulness of meetings
- Update meeting agendas ahead of time for 1:1s and project meetings
- Make sure to clarify who will do what and by when whenever a to-do comes up

## Mandatory Meetings

The list of recurring TPL meetings and their purpose is covered in onboarding checklists.

- All lab members are expected to attend team meetings
- Project meetings are mandatory during weeks when projects you are assigned to are discussed
- If you cannot attend a mandatory meeting, communicate with your manager and post in #lablogistics on Slack

## Partner Meeting Norms

- Most partner meetings are held virtually — arriving on time is critical
- If you will be late, notify the project Slack channel and your manager before the meeting start time
- Know who is responsible for the agenda and review all materials ahead of time
- The most junior person on the call should:
  - Come prepared with a note-taking document in the project's running notes folder
  - Note all names, titles, and affiliations of attendees
  - Be ready to link and share relevant documents during the meeting
- Meeting notes should include a clear record of all to-dos, next steps, and timelines discussed
- After the meeting, ensure all to-dos are tracked and followed up on by the agreed deadline

## Scheduling Elizabeth

Loop Caroline in for any meeting that involves Elizabeth. Make sure the following is clear:

- Length of meeting
- Frequency (one time or recurring)
- Type of meeting (in-person / phone / Zoom) and location if in-person
- Attendees and their email addresses
- When the meeting needs to happen by
- Purpose of the meeting
- Who is responsible for facilitating and setting the agenda
- Anything Elizabeth needs to review beforehand — must be provided at least 48 hours in advance with a note to Caroline

## Adding Things to Elizabeth's To-Do List

Caroline manages Elizabeth's to-do list. CC Caroline on any email or loop her into any Slack thread with a to-do for Elizabeth. Include:

- Context about where the to-do came from
- Links to any related documents
- The deadline and how it was set (Elizabeth generally needs one week's notice for review)
- What specifically is needed from Elizabeth
- The next step after Elizabeth's review
